text.p: Owu is a city, the king's name is Akinjobi and his wife is Erelu. Owu is a prosperous city and Ile-Ife is another yoruba land close to them. So the goddess Lawumi gave an order that no yoruba should sell other yoruba for slavery but owu people didn't obey the instruction, the Owu people waged war against Ile-Ife and took their men, took their men into slavery, one of the People person that Owu took from Ile-Ife was Inyuloye the wife of Okunade so they took her as a wife. So Lawumi was angry because the people of Owu didn't keep the sacred law, so she Okunade who was a vengeful man, that wanted to avenge not because of the love of his country but because of the love of a woman, Okunade left his art proffesion and joined the army and rose to the rank of a maye, so the allied forces broke into Owu city and killed all their men, meanwhile the king of Owu Akinjobi and his officers fled, the allied forces took over Owu land and sack the city of Owu.
Humour may be defined as the creation of laughter or it initates laughter.

Humour can be found in the case of Bluntschili the fugitive, a swiss machinery hired to fight for the Serbs against the Bulgarians rather to load his ammunitions with bullet rather it was loaded with chocolates that was why he was nicknamed "the chocolate cream soldier".

Another humour can be seen in the case of nicola (the man servant) who was embarrased by Catherine when he brought the luggages of bluntschili and it was seen by Mayor Petkoff, that was why Catherine was angry and she embarrassed him.

Another humour was when bluntschili came to return the coat, Raina shouted "the chocolate cream soldier" and Mayor Petkoff was suprised and asked Catherine and Raina who how the man came to be and who he was.
The poem the boy on the swing was written by Oswald Mtshali.
The poem is figuritive which means that it has a hidden meaning.
the They didn't told us who the boy is, but we know that somebody is on the swing.

There is a theme of poverty in one of the stanza's which says "His blue shirt billows in the breeze like a tattered kite" whe means that the tattered and the blue shirt he was wearing shows a sign of poverty. Meanwhile the blacks cannot afford long trousers only the white can afford the long trousers, that was why he was asking his history and asking in one of the stazans which says thus "When will I wear long trousers".
There is t loneliness, w because he has nothing doing so he went to the swing to pass out time. And he asked in one of the stanza's which says thus:"Why was my father jailed".

Tano: Tano is the great gods in a novel. When Pokuwa wants a child, the gods told her to bring back and egg so that she will bear a child. Anybody that have any problem will go to the gods for the problem to be solved.
Brenhoma: Brenhoma was known as a village in the novel and the people of brenhoma and the entire community of brenhoma were doing well.
When Pokuwa went to the farm and saw a dead body she shouted on top of her voice "The people of Brenhoma come and see what is happening in the village".

Santiago is a fisher man, and the name of the boy is Manolin, their relationship was a strong relationship. The two of them love and care for each other. Manolin even help the man in carrying his fish tools to his boat, Manolin also buys food s drinks for the man even when the boy is told not to follow the man to stream by his father parents, inspite of that he still obeys his parents but still he renders some help to the man in some ways.
